fact: September 27 sits exactly 270 days into the year - that's three-quarters of the way through!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this date falls smack in the middle of autumn's arrival, when leaves are going absolutely bonkers with color changes.
Squirrels across North America are in full panic mode right now, burying nuts like tiny furry preppers getting ready for winter.
The sun rises and sets at almost exactly the same time everywhere on Earth around this date - it's like nature's way of being fair to everyone!
September 27 is prime spider season in many places - house spiders are sneaking indoors to escape the cooling nights.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere spots, this date marks the sweet spot of spring when flowers are exploding into bloom.
Monarch butterflies are typically in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey south right now in North America - like tiny orange flying machines on the world's longest road trip.
Morning frost often starts appearing on grass in colder regions around this time, creating sparkly crystal carpets that melt by breakfast.
September 27 falls right in the middle of Libra season - the zodiac sign represented by scales, which is pretty perfect since day and night are nearly balanced.
Geese are typically honking their way south in massive V-formations right about now in northern climates, like feathered fighter jets following invisible highways in the sky.
